title: Automatic Cookie Handoff from WordPress to App Login Page
description:
Implement automatic cookie-based authentication handoff between the WordPress marketing site and the DataTools Pro Laravel application. When a user submits the beta signup form on the WordPress site, a cookie is set that allows them to bypass the passcode screen on the app.

The solution uses a shared `betapasscode` database table cloned from the Laravel app to WordPress. A Gravity Forms hook fires after successful form submission, retrieves a random passcode from the database, and sets a cross-subdomain cookie (`.datatoolspro.com`). The Laravel app validates this cookie against its own passcode table.

A region check was also implemented so that only US-based form submissions trigger the cookie bypass.
acceptance_criteria: Cookie is set on `.datatoolspro.com` domain after successful Gravity Form submission
Laravel app validates the cookie against the betapasscode table
Only US-region submissions trigger the passcode bypass
Cookie handoff works across WordPress and Laravel subdomains
middleware_changes: Gravity Forms hook sets a cross-subdomain cookie after form submission. Laravel middleware validates the cookie against the betapasscode table before granting access.
data_model_changes: Cloned `betapasscode` table from Laravel to WordPress database for shared passcode validation.
implementation_notes: WordPress Gravity Form hook (`gform_after_submission`) retrieves a random passcode and sets the `appdatatoolspro` cookie for `.datatoolspro.com`. Laravel middleware checks this cookie exists in the betapasscode table. Region check added to restrict to US submissions only.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the cookie handoff with WordPress Gravity Forms hook and Laravel middleware validation. Region check was added for US-only bypass. Product owner referenced MBT-332 for additional region check details.
